info.name: Grav CMS API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Grav CMS RESTful API plugin.

## Setup

1. Set the `base_url` variable to your Grav site URL (e.g., `https://localhost/grav-api`)
2. Set the `api_key` variable to a valid API key (generate via `bin/plugin api keys:generate`)
3. Set `grav_environment` to your Grav environment (default: `localhost`)
4. All requests include `X-API-Key` and `X-Grav-Environment` headers automatically

## Authentication

All requests (except `/auth/*` endpoints) require the `X-API-Key` header.
The `X-Grav-Environment` header is included on all requests to ensure correct environment context.

item.name: Setup
item.description: Setup requests that run before all other tests. Creates test data and authenticates.

request.description: Authenticate and save access_token and refresh_token to collection variables.
Create a test page used by subsequent tests. Saves the route to test_page_route.
Create a test webhook. Saves the webhook_id to collection variables.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Authentication endpoints. These do not require prior authentication.

request.description: Exchange username/password for JWT access and refresh tokens. The access token is automatically saved for use by other requests.
Exchange a refresh token for a new access/refresh token pair. The old refresh token is revoked (token rotation).
Revoke a refresh token, preventing it from being used to generate new access tokens.

item.name: Pages
item.description: Content management — create, read, update, delete, move, and copy pages.

request.description:
List all pages with filtering, sorting, and pagination.

**Filters** (enable as needed):
- `published` - true/false
- `template` - page template name
- `routable` - true/false
- `visible` - true/false
- `parent` - parent route prefix

**Sorting**: `sort` (date, title, slug, modified, order) + `order` (asc, desc)
Example: List published blog posts, newest first.
Get a single page by route. Returns full content, frontmatter, taxonomy, and media list.
Get a page with rendered HTML content and child pages up to 2 levels deep.
Create a new page. Requires `route` and `title`. Optional: `template`, `content`, `header` (frontmatter), `order`.

header.description: Optional: ETag from a previous GET for conflict detection

request.description:
Partial update — only send the fields you want to change. Supports `content`, `title`, `header`, `template`, `published`, `visible`.

Enable the `If-Match` header for optimistic concurrency control.
Move a page to a new parent and/or rename its slug. Requires `parent`. Optional: `slug`, `order`.
Copy a page (and its media) to a new route.

query.description: Set to true to also delete child pages

request.description: Delete a page. If the page has children and `children=true` is not set, returns a 422 error requiring confirmation.
List all taxonomy types (categories, tags, etc.) and their values across all pages.
Get only root-level pages (direct children of /). Returns has_children boolean for each page. Ideal for lazy-loading tree views and miller columns.

exec: pm.test('All pages are direct children of /blog', () => {

request.description: Get direct children of /blog only (one level deep). Unlike parent filter, children_of does not return deeper descendants.

item.name: Media
item.description: Media management — upload, list, and delete media files for pages and the site.

request.description: List all media files attached to a specific page.
Upload one or more media files to a page. Use multipart/form-data. Files are validated against Grav's security config for dangerous extensions.
Delete a specific media file from a page.

query.description: Recursive filename search across all subfolders
Filter by type: image, video, audio, document

request.description: List site-level media files and folders from user/media. Supports subfolder browsing via path, recursive search, and type filtering. Response includes folders array in meta.

query.description: Subfolder path to upload into (created automatically)

request.description: Upload media files to the site-level media folder (user/media). Optionally specify a subfolder path.
Delete a site-level media file. Filename may include a subfolder path (e.g. blog/hero.jpg).
Create a new folder in the site-level media directory.
Delete an empty folder from the site-level media directory.
Rename or move a media file within the site-level media directory.
Rename a folder within the site-level media directory.
Read the editable metadata (alt, title, caption, description) for a page media file, plus read-only stored keys.
Write editable metadata for a page media file. Only configured fields are changed; other sidecar keys are preserved. Values are sanitized.
Clear all configured metadata fields for a page media file. Technical keys are preserved.
Read editable metadata for a site-level media file, addressed by ?path=.
Write editable metadata for a site-level media file. Only configured fields are changed; other keys preserved.
Clear all configured metadata fields for a site-level media file.

item.name: Config
item.description: Configuration management — read and update system, site, plugin, and theme configs.

request.description: Read the system configuration.
Read the site configuration (site.yaml).
Read configuration for a specific plugin. Change `markdown` to any plugin name.
Read configuration for a specific theme.
Update site configuration. Values are deep-merged with existing config. Only send the fields you want to change.
Update a plugin's configuration.

item.name: Users
item.description: User account management and API key administration.

request.description: List all user accounts (paginated). Sensitive fields (password, API key hashes) are excluded.
Get details for a specific user.
Create a new user account. Required: `username`, `password`, `email`.
Partial update a user. Only send the fields you want to change.
List all API keys for a user. Returns metadata only (no hashes).
Generate a new API key. The raw key is returned ONCE — save it immediately. The key ID is saved to the `test_api_key_id` variable for the revoke request.
Revoke (delete) a specific API key.
Upload a custom avatar image for a user.
Remove the user's custom avatar.
Generate a new TOTP 2FA secret and return the QR code as a data URI.
Delete a user account. You cannot delete yourself.

item.name: System
item.description: System administration — info, cache, logs, and backups.

request.description: Lightweight health check endpoint. Returns {pong: true}. Useful for session keep-alive.
Get system information: Grav version, PHP version, extensions, plugins, themes, and environment.

query.description: Options: all, standard, images, assets, tmp

request.description: Clear the Grav cache. Scopes: `all`, `standard`, `images`, `assets`, `tmp`.
Clear ALL caches including images and assets.
Read Grav log entries. Optional `level` filter for error/warning/info/debug.
Create a new site backup.
List all available backups.

item.name: Languages
item.description: Site language configuration and multi-language support.

request.description: List all configured site languages and their status.

item.name: Pages / Multi-Language
item.description: Multi-language page operations — translations, language-specific content.

request.description: Get a page in a specific language.
List all available translations for a page.
Create a new translation for a page in a specific language.
Update a page in a specific language.
Delete a specific language file for a page.

item.name: Pages / Batch Operations
item.description: Batch page operations — reorder children, bulk publish, bulk delete.

request.description: Reorder child pages under a parent page.
Batch publish multiple pages at once.
Batch delete multiple pages at once.

exec: pm.test('Each result has route and slug', () => {

request.description: Reorganize multiple pages atomically — move across parents and/or reorder within the same request. Each operation specifies a page route and optionally a new parent and/or position. All operations are validated before any changes are applied.

item.name: GPM
item.description: Grav Package Manager — manage plugins, themes, updates, and the repository.

item.name: GPM / Installed
item.description: List and inspect installed plugins and themes.

request.description: List all installed plugins with their versions and status.
Get details for a specific installed plugin.
List all installed themes with their versions and status.
Get details for a specific installed theme.
Get the README.md content for an installed plugin.
Get the CHANGELOG.md content for an installed plugin.
Get the README.md content for an installed theme.
Get the CHANGELOG.md content for an installed theme.

item.name: GPM / Updates
item.description: Check for available updates.

request.description: Check for available updates to plugins, themes, and Grav core.
Check for updates with cache flushed to get the latest information.

item.name: GPM / Install & Remove
item.description: Install and remove packages.

request.description: Install a plugin from the GPM repository. For premium packages, add a `license` field: `"license": "XXXXXXXX-XXXXXXXX-XXXXXXXX-XXXXXXXX"`.
Install a theme from the GPM repository. For premium themes, add a `license` field: `"license": "XXXXXXXX-XXXXXXXX-XXXXXXXX-XXXXXXXX"`.
Remove an installed plugin or theme.
Install a package directly from a URL (zip file).

item.name: GPM / Update
item.description: Update packages and Grav core.

request.description: Update a specific package to the latest version.
Update all installed packages to their latest versions.
Upgrade Grav core to the latest version.

item.name: GPM / Repository
item.description: Browse and search the GPM repository for available packages.

query.description: Search query (matches slug, name, description, author, keywords)

request.description: Search across all plugins and themes in the GPM repository by keyword.

query.description: Optional keyword filter (matches slug, name, description, author, keywords)

request.description: Browse available plugins in the GPM repository. Use ?q=keyword to filter.

query.description: Optional keyword filter (matches slug, name, description, author, keywords)

request.description: Browse available themes in the GPM repository. Use ?q=keyword to filter.
Get details for a specific package in the GPM repository.

item.name: GPM / Plugin Pages
item.description: Plugin admin page definitions and custom web component scripts.

request.description: Get the admin page definition for a plugin. Returns page metadata, blueprint reference, data/save endpoints, and available actions.
Get the JavaScript web component script for a plugin's custom admin page. Returns application/javascript content.
Get the JavaScript web component script for a plugin's report. Returns application/javascript content. The reportId matches the component field from the /reports endpoint response.

item.name: Scheduler
item.description: Scheduler management — list jobs, view status, check history, and run the scheduler.

request.description: List all configured scheduler jobs.
Get the current scheduler status and cron configuration.
View the history of scheduler job executions.
Trigger the scheduler to run. Set force to true to run regardless of schedule.

item.name: System Info
item.description: System info and diagnostics overview.

request.description: Get system info overview including environment checks and diagnostics.

item.name: Reports
item.description: Plugin-extensible diagnostic reports.

request.description: Get plugin-extensible diagnostic reports including security check, YAML linting, and plugin-provided reports.

item.name: Sidebar
item.description: Admin sidebar navigation items contributed by plugins.

request.description: Get all admin sidebar navigation items contributed by installed plugins.

item.name: Dashboard
item.description: Dashboard data — notifications, news feed, and statistics.

request.description: Get all dashboard notifications.
Hide a specific notification by ID.
Get the dashboard news feed.
Get dashboard statistics (page counts, user counts, etc.).
Get page view statistics including daily chart data (last 14 days), summary counters, and top pages by views.

item.name: Webhooks
item.description: Webhook management — create, update, delete, and test webhooks.

request.description: List all configured webhooks.
Create a new webhook. The webhook ID is automatically saved to the `webhook_id` variable.
Get details for a specific webhook.
Update an existing webhook configuration.
Delete a webhook.
View delivery history for a specific webhook.
Send a test payload to a webhook endpoint.

item.name: Workflows
item.description: Multi-step workflow examples that chain API calls together.

request.description: List all available page template types in the active theme.
Get the resolved blueprint for the default page template. Includes all inherited fields from extends@ and import@ directives.
Get the resolved blueprint for the blog page template.
Get the configuration blueprint for the email plugin.
Get the configuration blueprint for the quark theme.
Get the blueprint for system configuration.
Get the user account form blueprint.
Get all registered permission actions as a nested tree.
Get the resolved blueprint for a plugin's custom admin page. Used to render blueprint-form type pages registered by plugins.
Resolve a blueprint data-options@ directive. Pass ?callable=\\Grav\\Common\\Page\\Pages::pageTypes
Get all translation strings for English. No authentication required.
Get translation strings filtered by PLUGIN_ADMIN prefix. Useful for partial/incremental loading.

item.name: Teardown
item.description: Teardown requests that run after all other tests. Cleans up test data.

request.description: Delete the test page created during setup.
Delete the test webhook created during setup.
Revoke the refresh token obtained during setup.
